name: core.openwop.data
description: Spec-canonical data-utility pack. Five pure, replay-safe nodes covering the most common workflow-author needs: string-case transformation, string splitting, array filtering, array mapping (with template substitution), and shallow object merging. Zero npm deps. Runs on any Node 20+ host advertising the openwop-node-packs profile.
author: OpenWOP Working Group

nodes.label: String Case
nodes.description: Transforms input string to upper / lower / title case. Pure, replay-safe, locale-agnostic (uses JavaScript built-in casing — title case is naive: first letter of each whitespace-separated word).

nodes.label: String Split
nodes.description: Splits input string on a separator. Pure. Returns an array. Empty separator splits into per-character array.

nodes.label: Array Filter
nodes.description: Filters an array by a simple equality predicate on a path. Pure. Predicate shape: { path: 'dot.notation', equals: <value> }. For richer predicate languages, downstream packs can layer (e.g., JSONPath or JMESPath as a future v1.x extension).

nodes.label: Array Map
nodes.description: Maps an array via a simple template — each element's keys are substituted into a `{{key}}` template. Pure. For richer transformation languages (jq-style, JSONata), downstream packs can layer.

nodes.label: Object Merge
nodes.description: Shallow-merges two objects. Later (`override`) wins on key collision. Pure. Deep-merge is intentionally out of scope; downstream packs can ship that variant.
